title: Use `Object.hasOwn(obj, key)` instead of `obj.hasOwnProperty(key)`
body:
Modern JS (ES2022) provides `Object.hasOwn(obj, key)` as a safer alternative to `obj.hasOwnProperty(key)`.

Why safer? If someone creates an object with `{ hasOwnProperty: 'lol' }`, calling `.hasOwnProperty()` on it will fail. `Object.hasOwn()` is a static method — immune to prototype pollution.

```js
// ❌ Risky
obj.hasOwnProperty('key')

// ✅ Safer
Object.hasOwn(obj, 'key')
```

title: Use `??` (nullish coalescing) instead of `||` for default values
body:
`||` treats `0`, `''`, `false`, `NaN` as falsy — usually not what you want for defaults.

`??` only treats `null` and `undefined` as nullish.

```js
const count = response.count || 10;     // ❌ 0 becomes 10
const count = response.count ?? 10;      // ✅ 0 stays 0
```

title: `Array.prototype.at()` for negative indexing
body:
Forget `arr[arr.length - 1]` — use `.at(-1)`.

```js
const arr = [10, 20, 30];
arr.at(-1);  // 30 (last element)
arr.at(-2);  // 20 (second-to-last)
arr.at(0);   // 10 (same as arr[0])
```

Works on strings too: `'hello'.at(-1)` → `'o'`.

title: Use `structuredClone()` for deep copies
Optional chaining `?.` is your friend
Use `Array.from()` with a mapper for clean transforms
Destructure with default values and renames
Use `Number.isFinite()` not `isFinite()`
body:
Global `isFinite()` coerces — `isFinite('42')` returns `true`. `Number.isFinite()` doesn't.

```js
isFinite('42');          // true (coerces string to number)
Number.isFinite('42');   // false (strict type check)
Number.isFinite(42);     // true
Number.isFinite(Infinity); // false
```

title: Bit-shift for integer division by powers of 2
body:
Bit-shifting is faster than `Math.floor(n / 2)`:

```js
Math.floor(7 / 2);   // 3
7 >> 1;              // 3 (same result, faster)

Math.floor(7 / 4);   // 1
7 >> 2;              // 1
```

Only works for positive integers and divisors that are powers of 2. Common in binary search midpoints.

title: Use `Set` for fast membership checks
`Promise.allSettled()` waits for all, never rejects
Use `AbortController` for cancellable fetch
Use `crypto.randomUUID()` for IDs, not `Math.random()`
Tagged template literals for safe HTML
Avoid `forEach` when you need to `break` or `return`
Use `??=` and `||=` for conditional assignment
Top-level `await` works in ES modules
`Object.entries()` for key-value iteration
Use `Intl` APIs for locale-aware formatting
body:
Stop hardcoding date/number formats. Use `Intl`:

```js
// Currency
new Intl.NumberFormat('en-IN', { style: 'currency', currency: 'INR' })
  .format(1234567.89);  // '₹12,34,567.89' (Indian grouping)

// Dates
new Intl.DateTimeFormat('en-IN', { dateStyle: 'full' })
  .format(new Date());  // 'Monday, 29 June 2026'

// Relative time
new Intl.RelativeTimeFormat('en', { style: 'short' })
  .format(-1, 'day');  // 'yesterday'
```
